title: Heaps: Priority First
subtitle: Always grab the most important item

sections.heading: What's a heap?
sections.body: A **heap** is a tree where the root is always the smallest (**min-heap**) or biggest (**max-heap**) value. It's how priority queues are built. Inserting and removing the top: O(log n).

sections.title: Hospital triage
sections.body: Most urgent patient is treated first, regardless of arrival time. A new patient comes in, gets ranked, and slots into the right priority. The most critical case is always on top — that's a heap.

questions.question: In a min-heap, what's always at the root?
questions.explanation: Min-heap = smallest on top. Max-heap = largest on top.
